title: Bug: Task reconciliation uses project_root as memory project_id
analysis:
The bug is a systematic conflation of two distinct identifiers: `project_root` (a filesystem path like `/home/leo/src/dark-factory` used by Taskmaster for task operations) and `project_id` (a logical identifier like `dark_factory` used by Graphiti/Mem0 for memory scoping). The conflation originates in `TaskInterceptor._make_event()` which receives `project_root` and passes it directly as `project_id` to `ReconciliationEvent`. This tainted value then flows through: (1) the event buffer (keyed by this path-as-id), (2) targeted reconciliation (passes it to both memory and task services), and (3) full pipeline stages via the harness (sets `stage.project_id` to the path). The `Scope` model then uses this path as `graphiti_group_id` and in `mem0_collection_name`, creating collections like `fused_/home/leo/src/dark-factory` instead of `fused_dark_factory`.

The fix introduces a `resolve_project_id(project_root)` function as the single conversion point, and threads both identifiers through all affected code paths: `project_id` (logical) for memory operations, `project_root` (filesystem) for task operations. The original `project_root` is preserved in event payloads under `_project_root` key so the full pipeline harness can extract it for stage task tools, avoiding any SQLite schema migration.

steps.description: Write tests for `resolve_project_id()` in new file `tests/test_scope.py`. Test cases: (1) `/home/leo/src/dark-factory` -> `dark_factory`, (2) trailing slash handling `/project/` -> `project`, (3) simple path `/project` -> `project`, (4) already-clean ID `my_project` passthrough, (5) path with multiple hyphens `/foo/my-cool-project` -> `my_cool_project`, (6) explicit mapping dict overrides derivation.

Implement `resolve_project_id(project_root: str, mapping: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> str` in `models/scope.py`. Logic: check mapping dict first, then derive from `PurePosixPath(project_root.rstrip('/')).name` with hyphens replaced by underscores and lowercased.

Write tests for TaskInterceptor event emission with resolved project_id. In `tests/test_task_interceptor.py`, add: (1) `test_event_project_id_is_resolved` - verify event in buffer has logical project_id (not filesystem path) after `set_task_status`, (2) `test_event_payload_contains_project_root` - verify event payload has `_project_root` key with original path, (3) `test_reconciler_receives_both_ids` - verify `reconciler.reconcile_task` is called with both `project_id` (logical) and `project_root` (path).

Update `TaskInterceptor._make_event()` to call `resolve_project_id(project_root)` for the event's `project_id` and inject `_project_root: project_root` into the payload dict. Update all `self.reconciler.reconcile_task()` calls to pass `project_root=project_root` alongside `project_id=resolve_project_id(project_root)`. Update `self.reconciler.reconcile_bulk_tasks()` calls similarly. Update existing tests that assert on event buffer stats to query by the resolved project_id instead of the raw path.

Write tests for TargetedReconciler using correct identifiers per service type. In `tests/test_targeted.py`, add: (1) `test_done_memory_ops_use_project_id` - verify `memory.add_memory` and `memory.search` are called with logical project_id (not path), (2) `test_done_task_ops_use_project_root` - verify `taskmaster.get_tasks` is called with filesystem project_root (not logical id), (3) `test_blocked_task_update_uses_project_root` - verify `taskmaster.update_task` for hints attachment uses project_root, (4) `test_bulk_reconcile_separates_ids` - verify `reconcile_bulk_tasks` uses project_id for memory.search and project_root for taskmaster calls.

Update `TargetedReconciler.reconcile_task()` to accept `project_root: str` parameter alongside existing `project_id`. Thread `project_root` through to `_on_task_done`, `_on_task_blocked`, `_on_task_cancelled`, `_on_task_deferred` handlers. In handlers, use `project_id` for all `self.memory.*` calls and `project_root` for all `self.taskmaster.*` calls. Similarly update `reconcile_bulk_tasks()` to accept and use `project_root`.

Write tests for BaseStage tool separation. In `tests/test_targeted.py` or a new `tests/test_stages.py`, test that: (1) `_memory_read_tools` closures use `self.project_id` for search/get_entity/get_episodes calls, (2) `_task_read_tools` closures use `self.project_root` for get_tasks/get_task calls, (3) `_task_write_tools` closures use `self.project_root` for set_task_status/update_task calls, (4) `_memory_write_tools` closures use `self.project_id` for add_memory/delete_memory calls.

Update `BaseStage` to add `project_root: str = ''` attribute alongside existing `project_id`. Update `_task_read_tools()` and `_task_write_tools()` to use `self.project_root` instead of `self.project_id` for all `taskmaster.*` calls. Memory tool methods remain using `self.project_id`.

Write test for ReconciliationHarness setting both identifiers on stages. In `tests/test_harness.py`, add: (1) `test_full_cycle_extracts_project_root_from_events` - push events with `_project_root` in payload, run full cycle (mocked stages), verify both `stage.project_id` and `stage.project_root` are set correctly on each stage.

Update `ReconciliationHarness.run_full_cycle()` to extract `project_root` from drained events' payloads (first event with `_project_root` key). After draining, set both `stage.project_id = project_id` and `stage.project_root = project_root` for each stage. Handle case where project_root is not in payload (fallback to project_id for backwards compatibility).

Write integration test: end-to-end event flow from TaskInterceptor through buffer and back. In `tests/test_task_interceptor.py`, add `test_event_roundtrip_preserves_both_ids` - create interceptor, call set_task_status with a filesystem path, drain events from buffer, verify: event.project_id is logical, event.payload['_project_root'] is the original path, buffer stats queryable by logical project_id.

Update all existing tests across test_task_interceptor.py, test_targeted.py, and test_harness.py to use resolved project_ids where they assert on buffer stats or event project_id values. Ensure full test suite passes with `pytest fused-memory/tests/`.

design_decisions.decision: Pure function `resolve_project_id()` in models/scope.py as the single conversion point
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the mapping logic co-located with the Scope model that maps project_id to backend identifiers. A pure function (not a method) is easily testable and callable from any context without instantiation. Accepts optional mapping dict for explicit overrides without requiring config dependency.

design_decisions.decision: Store project_root in event payload under `_project_root` key rather than adding a column to the event_buffer SQLite schema
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids SQLite schema migration complexity. The payload dict is already JSON-serialized and round-tripped through the buffer. The underscore prefix signals it's system metadata, not user-supplied. Backwards compatible: events without the key are handled gracefully.

design_decisions.decision: Default derivation uses Path.name with hyphens replaced by underscores
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the established convention: project_root `/home/leo/src/dark-factory` maps to project_id `dark_factory`. Hyphens are problematic in Qdrant collection names and Graphiti group IDs. Lowercasing ensures consistency.

design_decisions.decision: TargetedReconciler and BaseStage receive both project_id and project_root as separate parameters/attributes
design_decisions.rationale: Makes the dual-identity explicit in the API rather than hiding it behind a lookup. Each call site clearly shows which identifier is used for which service. The TaskInterceptor resolves once and passes both down.

design_decisions.decision: Harness extracts project_root from drained events with fallback to project_id
design_decisions.rationale: Graceful degradation: events created before the fix (without `_project_root` in payload) still work — the harness falls back to using project_id for task operations, which was the old behavior. New events carry both identifiers.

reuse.where: Already maps project_id to graphiti_group_id and mem0_collection_name
reuse.how: Co-locate resolve_project_id() in the same module since it produces the project_id that Scope consumes. No changes to Scope itself needed — the fix is upstream.

reuse.what: EventBuffer push/drain round-trip via payload JSON
reuse.where: reconciliation/event_buffer.py already serializes payload to JSON on push and deserializes on drain
reuse.how: Leverage existing payload serialization to carry _project_root through the buffer without schema changes.

reuse.what: Existing test fixtures (taskmaster, reconciler, event_buffer, mock_memory_service)
reuse.how: Reuse existing AsyncMock fixtures; add assertions for the new project_root parameter on mock calls.
The existing closure pattern that captures self.project_id already works — just need to add self.project_root and update task tool closures to capture that instead.
